frame: Motherhood as Basis of Respect, Women Defined by Male Relations
generated_comment: Geeta ji, aap toh bahut lucky hain — bahu ne ghar ko ghar banaya. Yeh Priya ka toh pata nahi... chha saal ho gaye, koi baccha nahi, bas office office. Pati bechara kya sochta hoga? Aurat ki asli izzat toh ghar se milti hai, company ke ID card se nahi. Ek din pachhtaayegi, jab umar nikal jaayegi aur haath mein sirf promotion hogi, godi mein koi nahi hoga.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: The comment encodes the belief that a woman's respect and worth are contingent on motherhood and domesticity, framing career as a selfish deviation from her 'real' role; it also centres the husband's perspective ('pati bechara') to judge Priya's choices, reflecting the 'Women Defined by Male Relations' frame. The tone is sympathetic and cautionary rather than openly hostile, making it covert misogyny.
persona_text: You are Sunita. Age 44. Married. A homemaker in Jaipur. Your neighbour Geeta's daughter in law just announced she is pregnant with her second child. Geeta is overjoyed. You are sitting with Geeta and two other neighbourhood women having tea. The conversation turns to another woman in the colony Ñ Priya Ñ who has been married six years, has no children, and works at a private company. You are giving your opinion on Priya's situation and what you think her priorities are and what a woman who chooses career over children is really choosing.
